metadata.title: Product Bricks & Capabilities
metadata.description: Hierarchical product brick map for the ride-sharing-marketplace domain.

bricks.name: Trip Request and Intent Capture
bricks.description: Captures rider pickup, destination, timing, ride type, account context, and trip intent before dispatch begins.

links.label: The Architecture of Schemaless, Uber Engineering's Trip Datastore Using MySQL
links.note: Explains the distributed trip datastore architecture behind core trip state.

links.label: Using Triggers On Schemaless, Uber Engineering's Datastore Using MySQL
links.note: Shows how trip lifecycle changes feed billing, workflow, and downstream processing.

internalModules.title: Request Capture and Validation
internalModules.description: Validates trip inputs, eligibility, and account context.

internalModules.title: Product Selection Logic
internalModules.description: Determines which ride products and variants are offered for the requested trip.

internalModules.title: Request State Management
internalModules.description: Stores request lifecycle state before, during, and after dispatch.

interfaces.title: Trip Request API
interfaces.description: API for creating and updating ride requests.

interfaces.title: Request UX Surface
interfaces.description: Rider-facing request flow in mobile surfaces.

interfaces.title: Request Events
interfaces.description: Event stream for request creation, update, and cancellation.

brickDependencies.description: Depends on Mobile App Experience Platform for realtime client state, analytics instrumentation, and resilient trip-request surfaces.
Depends on Client API Composition and Server-Driven Experience to assemble rider-facing view models and action contracts without forcing mobile apps to own marketplace orchestration details.
Depends on Dynamic Pricing and Incentives for upfront fare and offer packaging.
Depends on Rider Identity, Wallet and Payments for account, wallet, and billing context.

externalSystemsThisBrickDependsOn.description: Supports address normalization and pickup or destination interpretation.

externalSystemsDependingOnThisBrick.description: Rider clients depend on request capture to start trips.

dataDependencies.description: Acts as the primary owning brick for Customer Profile.
Writes or updates Customer Contact Point during domain workflows.
Reads Consent Record to support domain workflows.
Acts as the primary owning brick for Trip Request And Intent Capture.

bricks.name: ETA, Maps and Routing Intelligence
bricks.description: Provides route estimation, pickup guidance, airport geofencing, and live map context for riders and drivers.

links.label: ETA Phone Home: How Uber Engineers an Efficient Route
links.note: Covers an in-house routing and ETA stack for pickup and trip-time estimation at marketplace scale.

links.label: How We Built Uber Engineering's Highest Query per Second Service Using Go
links.note: Explains the geofence service used for geo-based product availability, airports, and surge zones.

links.label: Improving Uber's Mapping Accuracy with CatchME
links.note: Shows how mapping feedback loops improve route quality and ETA accuracy.

internalModules.title: ETA Prediction Models

internalModules.title: Pickup and Drop-off Guidance

internalModules.description: Maintains airport, venue, and city-specific navigation constraints.

interfaces.description: Returns routes, ETAs, and pickup guidance.

interfaces.title: Live Location Events
interfaces.description: Emits location and route progress events.

brickDependencies.description: Depends on Service Runtime and Reliability Foundation for low-latency routing calls, failover, and resilient map-serving paths.

dataDependencies.description: Acts as the primary owning brick for Eta Maps And Routing Intelligence.

bricks.name: Rider Identity, Wallet and Payments
bricks.description: Manages rider identity, family or business profiles, payment methods, receipts, refunds, and billing context.

links.label: Building High Throughput Payment Account Processing
links.note: Describes hot-account financial processing and audit-safe money movement in a large payments platform.

links.label: Use Passkeys Wherever You Sign in to Uber
links.note: Explains account authentication architecture for lower-friction, stronger identity security.

internalModules.title: Identity and Account Graph

bricks.name: Support Automation and Issue Resolution
bricks.description: Handles in-app support entry, issue classification, self-service refunds or corrections, agent context assembly, and trip-resolution workflows for riders and drivers.

links.label: Architecting Uber Support with Customer Obsession Engineering
links.note: Explains an in-app support framework, self-service flows, agent tooling, and issue routing model.

internalModules.title: Issue Intake and Classification
internalModules.description: Captures issue type, trip context, and structured resolution intent from riders and drivers.

internalModules.title: Self-Service Resolution Engine
internalModules.description: Applies fixed rules and workflow components for refunds, adjustments, ratings, and trip corrections.

internalModules.title: Agent Context and Routing Workspace
internalModules.description: Assembles trip, payment, safety, and account context for faster routing and human resolution.

interfaces.title: Support Workflow API
interfaces.description: API for issue creation, self-service decisions, and case state transitions.

interfaces.title: In-App Help Surface
interfaces.description: Rider and driver self-service support experience across mobile and web.

brickDependencies.description: Depends on Trip Request and Intent Capture for trip-level issue context and lifecycle history.
Depends on Rider Identity, Wallet and Payments for receipts, adjustments, and refund execution.
Depends on Safety and Trust Operations for incidents, escalations, and sensitive trust workflows.
Depends on Marketplace Intelligence and Analytics for support routing feedback and resolution-quality measurement.
Depends on Client API Composition and Server-Driven Experience to render contextual help entry points, issue panels, and server-controlled support actions consistently across apps.

externalSystemsDependingOnThisBrick.description: Mobile and web clients depend on structured issue submission and self-service resolution flows.

dataDependencies.description: Reads Trip Request And Intent Capture to assemble issue context and support workflows.
Reads Rider Identity Wallet And Payments to support receipt lookup and financial issue handling.
Reads Safety And Trust Operations to route incidents into trust and safety workflows.
Publishes support-case actions and resolution records to Audit Log.

bricks.name: Client API Composition and Server-Driven Experience
bricks.description: Provides mobile-focused backend-for-frontend composition, decomposed client APIs, server-driven view and action contracts, and AI-assisted product prototyping workflows for trip, support, and special marketplace flows.

links.label: AI Prototyping Is Changing How We Build Products at Uber
links.note: Shows how product, design, engineering, operations, policy, and legal can align faster around working prototypes.

links.label: How Uber Built an Agentic System to Automate Design Specs in Minutes
links.note: Supports AI-assisted design-to-engineering handoff and faster specification of client-facing product changes.

links.label: Decomposing network calls on the Lyft mobile apps
links.note: Describes a move away from monolithic mobile endpoints toward decomposed APIs better aligned with client concerns.

links.label: The Journey to Server Driven UI At Lyft Bikes and Scooters
links.note: Shows a move from business models to BFF-driven view models, reusable components, and server-controlled actions.

internalModules.title: Backend for Frontend Orchestration
internalModules.description: Aggregates trip, pricing, identity, and routing state into client-facing responses built for app flows rather than backend ownership boundaries.

internalModules.title: Server-Driven Components and Actions
internalModules.description: Defines reusable view models, semantic components, and action contracts so product changes can ship with less app-release coupling.

internalModules.title: Client Contract Decomposition Layer
internalModules.description: Breaks large mobile responses into focused contracts and capability-gated payloads that are easier to evolve safely across rider and driver apps.

internalModules.title: AI-Assisted Experience Prototyping
internalModules.description: Turns product concepts, flow requirements, and design-system constraints into reviewable prototypes and implementation-ready experience specifications.

interfaces.title: Client Experience API
interfaces.description: Mobile-facing API layer returning flow-specific view models, actions, and composed state.

interfaces.title: View Model Schema
interfaces.description: Schema contract for server-driven panels, actions, and capability-aware mobile rendering.

brickDependencies.description: Depends on Trip Request and Intent Capture for rider intent, trip lifecycle state, and demand context.
Depends on ETA, Maps and Routing Intelligence for pickup guidance, route context, and trip-state presentation.

Depends on Mobile App Experience Platform for client capability signals, realtime synchronization, and mobile runtime integration.

dataDependencies.description: Reads Trip Request And Intent Capture to build client-facing trip and demand experiences.

Publishes composed action execution and contract-level diagnostics to Audit Log.
